subject: History
chapter: Bricks, Beads and Bones: The Harappan Civilisation

questions.q: What were the major features of the Harappan urban planning?
questions.answer: The major features of the Harappan urban planning were grid-patterned roads, public baths, and a well-planned drainage system.

questions.q: What was the significance of the Indus script?
questions.answer: The Indus script was significant because it is one of the earliest forms of writing in the world and has not been deciphered yet.

questions.q: Who was the ruler of the Harappan city of Mohenjo-Daro?
questions.answer: _____(Name of the ruler not mentioned in the NCERT textbook)

questions.q: What was the role of women in the Harappan society?
questions.answer: Women in the Harappan society had a significant role in the economy and trade, and were also buried with their husbands in the cemetery.

questions.q: What was the significance of the Great Bath at Mohenjo-Daro?
questions.answer: The Great Bath at Mohenjo-Daro was significant because it was a well-planned public bath with a sophisticated drainage system.

questions.q: Who were the artisans in the Harappan society?
questions.answer: _____(Type of artisans not mentioned in the NCERT textbook)

questions.q: What were the major crops grown in the Indus Valley region?
questions.answer: The major crops grown in the Indus Valley region were wheat, barley, and cotton.

questions.q: What was the significance of the seals in the Harappan society?
questions.answer: The seals in the Harappan society were significant because they were used for trade and commerce, and also served as a means of identification.

questions.q: Who were the rulers of the Harappan cities?
questions.answer: _____(Type of rulers not mentioned in the NCERT textbook)

questions.q: What was the significance of the urban planning in the Harappan cities?
questions.answer: The urban planning in the Harappan cities was significant because it was well-planned, with a grid-patterned road system and a sophisticated drainage system.
